description: Fires when the hash in the page URL changes or when the browser history object changes (pushState, replaceState, popState, hashchange). Used mainly for single-page applications (SPAs) where there are no full page reloads. By default the tag fires on every history change; add conditions to fire only for specific pages or history events (e.g. History source, History fragment, Page URL). See Piwik PRO help: History change trigger.

condition_types.description: Fires when a history or hash change occurs on the selected page URL
Fires when a history or hash change occurs on a page with the selected path
Fires when a history or hash change occurs on the selected hostname
Fires when a specific history event occurs (pushState, replaceState, popState or hashchange)
Fires when the URL hash matches the selected condition
Fires when the previous URL hash matches the selected condition
Fires when the history state matches the selected condition
condition_types.example: History state is set or History state equals my-state

condition_types.description: Fires when the previous history state matches the selected condition
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected internal referrer
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected external referrer
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected campaign (utm_campaign)
Fires when traffic source matches (campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct)
Fires when the visitor is a returning visitor and a history or hash change occurs
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Track virtual page views or route changes in single-page applications (SPAs)
Fire tags when the URL hash or history API changes without a full reload
Capture SPA navigation that would otherwise be missed by a standard page view trigger
common_use_cases: SPA route change tracking (hash or History API)
best_practices: For SPAs, consider using the debounced history trigger (trigger_type debounced_history) with wait for more accurate page-title matching and to avoid duplicate events from rapid navigations
Add conditions (e.g. Page path, History fragment) to fire only on relevant route changes
Use History source condition to fire only on pushState vs hashchange if needed

create_trigger.description: Create a history change trigger. Fires on every hash or history change unless conditions narrow it.

trigger_type.description: Must be 'history' for history change triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects; optional. Use to restrict to specific Page URL, Page path, History source, History fragment, etc.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing history change trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Optional; when empty, fires on every history/hash change. Use Page URL, Page path, History source, History fragment, Referrer, Campaign, etc.
conditions.recommendation: For SPAs, add Page path or History fragment conditions to avoid firing on every navigation, or use debounced_history for delayed firing.

spa_all_history_example.description: Fire on every history or hash change (no conditions)

history_fragment_example.description: Fire only when history fragment equals pricing

common_mistakes: Using history trigger on non-SPA sites where page view trigger is sufficient
Firing on every change without conditions and causing excessive tag fires in SPAs
Expecting page titles to match immediately in SPAs – use debounced_history with wait for better alignment

possible_causes: SPA does not use hash or History API
debugging_steps: Verify the app triggers hashchange or pushState/replaceState
